assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN (eval-3 at 85% pass rate, 2 failing assertions on convention upgrade eligibility); Repetitive Test Detection: N/A; Test Documentation: N/A'. The Test Quality verdict is WARN, with the Eval Quality component being WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: In review-40001.md: 'Classification: Eval Result Review (excluded from comment classification)' with detection via 3-criteria heuristic (author=github-actions[bot], body contains '## Eval Results', body contains 'sdlc-workflow/run-evals'). The report.md 'Eval Result Detection' section (lines 27-31) confirms the eval review was detected. Test Quality row in report.md (line 13) reflects eval metrics: 'eval-3 at 85% pass rate, 2 failing assertions on convention upgrade eligibility'. review-40001.md (lines 23-37) lists per-eval pass rates and failing assertion details extracted from the eval review.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: In review-50001.md: 'Classification: Code Change Request' with reasoning based on reviewer language analysis ('should still verify', 'CHANGES_REQUESTED' state). The file explicitly states: 'This is NOT an eval result -- it is a human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (a non-bot user with id 10002), attached to review 40002 which has state CHANGES_REQUESTED. It must be processed through the normal review comment classification pipeline.' The classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics. In the report.md Review Comment Processing table (lines 36-39), comment 50001 is classified as 'Code change request' while review 40001 is separately classified as 'Eval result review (excluded)'.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: Report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN (eval-3 at 85% pass rate, 2 failing assertions on convention upgrade eligibility)'. The failing assertion details about convention upgrade eligibility appear in the Test Quality row. Further details about the two specific assertions (convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation) are elaborated in the report body (lines 22-23): 'The verify-pr skill does not document convention upgrade eligibility reasoning in classification outputs, and does not create sub-tasks for suggestions that match project conventions.'

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md is the eval failure sub-task for eval-3. It targets 'eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation for review comment 30002'. It includes both failing assertions with their evidence: Assertion 1 (convention upgrade eligibility) with evidence 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility' and Assertion 2 (sub-task creation) with evidence 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md has a '## Review Context' section (lines 28-39) that includes: 'Source: Eval result review from CI (github-actions[bot], review ID 40001)', 'Eval ID: eval-3', 'Pass Rate: 85% (11/13 passed, 2 failed)'. It quotes Failing Assertion 1 text and evidence about convention upgrade eligibility, and Failing Assertion 2 text and evidence about sub-task creation. The evidence text explains what was missing: 'no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented' and 'no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 33: '## Target PR' followed by line 34: 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-2.md line 41: '## Target PR' followed by line 42: 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. Both sub-task descriptions include the Target PR section with the correct URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: Report.md line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Investigated 2 sub-tasks: reviewer feedback sub-task (Markdown documentation rule) and eval failure sub-task (convention upgrade eligibility). Root-cause analysis completed for both.' The verdict is 'DONE' (not N/A). The Root-Cause Investigation section (lines 48-57) details the investigation for both sub-tasks, including Sub-task 2 (eval-3 convention upgrade eligibility) which was classified as 'Universal knowledge, method-based' with root cause analysis identifying the implement-task skill gap.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only verification outputs: report.md (verification report), review-50001.md and review-40001.md (review classifications), subtask-1.md and subtask-2.md (sub-task descriptions), and criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md (acceptance criteria evaluations). All files are analysis/reporting artifacts. No code files (.ts, .js, .py, .md skill files, etc.) appear in the outputs. The report itself confirms the PR modifies 'exactly the 2 files specified in the task' (line 7), and the skill's outputs are strictly read/verify/report/sub-task artifacts.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: Report.md shows independent non-eval checks: Scope Containment (line 7): 'PASS | PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task: plugins/sdlc-workflow/skills/verify-pr/style-conventions.md and plugins/sdlc-workflow/skills/verify-pr/SKILL.md' -- compares diff files against task. Sensitive Patterns (line 10): 'PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in the diff' -- scans diff independently. Acceptance Criteria (line 12): 'PASS | 7/7 criteria met -- Check 6 scans for new symbols, verifies doc comments using language conventions, produces correct PASS/WARN/N/A verdicts, Output Format includes sixth row, Step 6a mapping includes Documentation Coverage' -- each criterion (criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md) evaluates against the diff content with no reference to eval results. None of these checks reference eval metrics, eval pass rates, or eval assertion failures.
